author: John Chrysostom

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

source.title: NPNF1-14. Saint Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of St. John and the Epistle to the Hebrews

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Then came the officers to the Chief Priests and Pharisees; and they said unto them, Why have ye not brought him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The officers answered, Never man spake like this Man.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: There is nothing clearer, nothing simpler than the truth, if we deal not perversely; just as (on the other hand) if we deal perversely, nothing is more difficult.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For behold, the Scribes and Pharisees, who seemed forsooth to be wiser than other men, being ever with Christ for the sake of plotting against Him, and beholding His miracles, and reading the Scriptures, were nothing profited, but were even harmed; while the officers, who could not claim one of these privileges, were subdued by one single sermon, and they who had gone forth to bind Him, came back bound themselves by wonder.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: We must not only marvel at their understanding, that they needed not signs, but were taken by the teaching alone; (for they said not, “Never man wrought miracles thus,” but, “Never man spake thus”;) we must not, I say, merely marvel at their understanding, but also at their boldness, that they spake thus to those that had sent them, to the Pharisees, to His enemies, to men who were doing all with a view to gratify their enmity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “The officers,” saith the Evangelist, “came, and the Pharisees said unto them, Why have ye not brought him?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: To “come” was a far greater deed than to have remained, for in the latter case they would have been rid of the annoyance of these men, but now they become heralds of the wisdom of Christ, and manifested their boldness in greater degree.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And they say not, “We could not become of the multitude, for they gave heed unto Him as unto a prophet”; but what?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Never man spake as this Man.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet they might have alleged that, but they show their right feeling.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For theirs was the saying not only of men admiring Him, but blaming their masters, because they had sent them to bind Him whom it behooved rather to hear.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet they had not heard a sermon either, but a short one; for when the long mind is impartial, there is no need of long arguments.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Such a thing is truth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then say the Pharisees?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When they ought to have been pricked at the heart, they, on the contrary, retort a charge on the officers, saying,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Are ye also deceived?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They still speak them fair, and do not express themselves harshly, dreading lest the others should entirely separate themselves, yet nevertheless they give signs of anger, and speak sparingly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For when they ought to have asked what He spake, and to have marveled at the words, they do not so, (knowing that they might have been captivated,) but reason with them from a very foolish argument;

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Wherefore,” saith one, “hath none of the rulers believed on Him?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Dost thou then make this a charge against Christ, tell me, and not against the unbelievers?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But the people,” saith one, “which knoweth not the Law, are accursed.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then is the charge against you the heavier, because the people believed, and ye believed not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They acted like men that knew the Law; how then are they accursed?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It is ye that are accursed, who keep not the Law, not they, who obey the Law.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Neither was it right, on the evidence of unbelievers, to slander one in whom they believed not, for this is an unjust mode of acting.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For ye also believed not God, as Paul saith; “What if some did not believe? shall their unbelief make the faith of God of none effect?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: God forbid.” ( Rom. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the Prophets ever rebuked them, saying, “Hear, ye rulers of Sodom”; and, “Thy rulers are disobedient” ( Isa. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 10, 23 ); and again, “Is it not for you to know judgment?” ( Mic. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And everywhere they attack them vehemently.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Shall one blame God for this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Away with the thought.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This blame is theirs.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And what other proof can a man bring of your not knowing the Law than your not obeying it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For when they had said, “Hath any of the rulers believed on him?” and, “These who know not the Law,” Nicodemus in fair consequence upbraids them, saying,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Doth our law judge any man before it hear him?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He showeth that they neither know the Law, nor do the Law; for if that Law commandeth to kill no man without first hearing him, and they before hearing were eager for this deed, they were transgressors of the Law.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And because they said, “None of the rulers hath believed on him” ( ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 50 ), therefore the Evangelist informs us that Nicodemus was “one of them,” to show that even rulers believed on Him; for although they showed not yet fitting boldness, still they were becoming attached to Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Observe how cautiously he rebukes them; he said not, “Ye desire to kill him, and condemn the man for a deceiver without proof”; but spake in a milder way, hindering their excessive violence, and their inconsiderate and murderous disposition.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore he turns his discourse to the Law, saying, “Except it hear him carefully, and know what he doeth.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So that not a bare “hearing,” but “careful hearing” is required.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the meaning of, “know what he doeth,” is, “what he intendeth,” “on what account,” “for what purpose,” “whether for the subversion of the order of things and as an enemy.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Being therefore perplexed, because they had said, “None of the rulers hath believed on him,” they addressed him, neither vehemently, nor yet with forbearance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For tell me, after he had said, “The Law judgeth no man,” how doth it follow that they should say,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Art thou also of Galilee?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When they ought to have shown that they had not sent to summon Him without judgment, or that it was not fitting to allow Him speech, they take the reply rather in a rough and angry manner.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Search, and look: for out of Galilee hath arisen no prophet.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why, what had the man said? that Christ was a prophet?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: No; he said, that He ought not to be slain unjudged; but they replied insolently, and as to one who knew nothing of the Scriptures; as though one had said, “Go, learn,” for this is the meaning of, “Search, and look.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then did Christ?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since they were continually dwelling upon Galilee and “The Prophet,” to free all men from this erroneous suspicion, and to show that He was not one of the prophets, but the Master of the world, He said,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Chap. viii. ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “I am the light of the world.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Not “of Galilee,” not of Palestine, nor of Judæa.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then say the Jews?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Thou bearest record of thyself, thy record is not true.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Alas! for their folly, He continually referred them to the Scriptures, and now they say, “Thou bearest record of thyself.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What was the record He bare?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “I am the light of the world.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: A great thing to say, great of a truth, but it did not greatly amaze them, because He did not now make Himself equal to the Father, nor assert that He was His Son, nor that He was God, but for a while calleth Himself “a light.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They indeed desired to disprove this also, and yet this was a much greater thing than to say,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “He that followeth Me, shall not walk in darkness.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Using the words “light” and “darkness” in a spiritual sense, and meaning thereby “abideth not in error.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: In this place He draweth on Nicodemus, and bringeth him in as having spoken very boldly, and praiseth the servants who had also done so.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For to “cry aloud,” is the act of one desirous to cause that they also should hear.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: At the same time He hinteth at these who were secretly contriving treacheries, being both in darkness and error, but that they should not prevail over the light.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And He remindeth Nicodemus of the words which He had uttered before, “Every one that doeth evil hateth the light, neither cometh to the light, lest his deeds should be reproved.” ( c. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For since they had asserted that none of the rulers had believed on Him, therefore He saith, that “he that doeth evil cometh not to the light,” to show that their not having come proceedeth not from the weakness of the light, but from their own perverse will.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “They answered and said unto Him, Dost thou bear witness to thyself?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Though I bear record of Myself, My record is true; for I know whence I come, and whither I go; but ye cannot tell whence I come.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What He had before said, these men bring forward as if it had been specially asserted.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then doth Christ?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: To refute this, and to show that He used those expressions as suitable to them and to their suspicions, who supposed Him to be a mere man, He saith, “Though I bear record of Myself, My record is true, for I know whence I come.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What is this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “I am of God, am God, the Son of God, and God Himself is a faithful witness unto Himself, but ye know Him not; ye willingly err, knowing ye pretend not to know, but say all that ye say according to mere human imagination, choosing to understand nothing beyond what is seen.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Ye judge after the flesh.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As to live after the flesh is to live badly, so to judge after the flesh is to judge unjustly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But I judge no man.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And yet if I judge, My judgment is true.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What He saith, is of this kind; “Ye judge unjustly.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And if,” saith some one, “we judge unjustly, why dost Thou not rebuke us? why dost Thou not punish us? why dost Thou not condemn us?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Because,” He saith, “I came not for this.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This is the meaning of, “I judge no man; yet if I judge, My judgment is true.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “For had I been willing to judge, ye would have been among the condemned.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this I say, not judging you.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet neither do I tell you that I say it, not judging you, as though I were not confident that had I judged you, I should have convicted you; since if I had judged you, I must justly have condemned you.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But now the time of judgment is not yet.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He alluded also to the judgment to come, saying,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “I am not alone, but I and the Father that sent Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Here He hinted, that not He alone condemneth them, but the Father also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then He concealed this, by leading them to His own testimony.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “It is written in your Law, that the testimony of two men is true.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What would the heretics say here?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: (They would say,) “How is he better than man, if we take what he hath said simply?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For this rule is laid down in the case of men, because no man by himself is trustworthy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But in the case of God, how can one endure such a mode of speaking?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How then is the word ‘two’ used?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Is it because they are two, or because being men they are therefore two?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If it is because they are two, why did he not betake himself to John, and say, I bear witness of myself, and John beareth witness of me?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore not to the angels?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore not to the prophets?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For he might have found ten thousand other testimonies.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But he desireth to show not this only that there are Two, but also that they are of the same Substance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Then said they unto Him, Who is thy father?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Jesus answered, Ye neither know Me, nor My Father.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because while they knew they spake as though they knew not, and as if trying Him, He doth not even deem them worthy of an answer.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore henceforth He speaketh all more clearly and more boldly; drawing His testimony from signs, and from His teaching of them that followed Him, and by the Cross being near.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For, “I know,” He saith, “whence I come.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This would not greatly affect them, but the adding, “and whither I go,” would rather terrify them, since He was not to remain in death.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But why said He not, “I know that I am God,” instead of, “I know whence I come”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He ever mingleth lowly words with sublime, and even these He veileth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For after saying, “I bear witness of Myself,” and proving this, He descendeth to a humbler strain.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As though He had said, “I know from whom I am sent, and to whom I depart.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For so they could have had nothing to say against it, when they heard that He was sent from Him, and would depart to Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “I could not have spoken,” He saith, “any falsehood, I who am come from thence, and depart thither, to the true God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But ye know not God, and therefore judge according to the flesh.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if having heard so many sure signs and proofs ye still say, ‘thy witness is not true,’ if ye deem Moses worthy of credit, both as to what he speaketh concerning others and what he speaketh concerning himself, but Christ not so, this is to judge according to the flesh.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But I judge no man.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He saith indeed also that “the Father judgeth no man.” ( c. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How then doth He here declare, that, “If I judge, My judgment is just, for I am not alone”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He again speaketh in reply to their thoughts.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “The judgment which is Mine is the judgment of the Father.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The Father, judging, would not judge otherwise than as I do, and I should not judge otherwise than as the Father.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore did He mention the Father?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because they would not have thought that the Son was to be believed unless He received the witness of the Father.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Besides, the saying doth not even hold good.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For in the case of men when two bear witness in a matter pertaining to another, then their witness is true, (this is for two to witness,) but if one should witness for himself, then they are no longer two.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou that He said this for nothing else but to show that He was of the same Substance, that He needed no other witness, and was in nothing inferior to the Father?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Observe at least His independence ;

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “I am One that bear witness of Myself; and the Father that sent Me beareth witness of Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Had He been of inferior substance, He would not have put this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But now that thou mayest not deem that the Father is included, to make up the number (of two), observe that His power hath nothing different (from the Father’s).

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: A man bears witness when he is trustworthy of himself, not when he himself needs testimony, and that too in a matter pertaining to another; but in a matter of his own, where he needs the witness of another, he is not trustworthy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But in this case it is all contrary.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For He though bearing witness in a matter of His own, and saying that witness is borne to Him by another, asserteth that He is trustworthy, in every way manifesting His independence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For why, when He had said, “I am not alone, but I and the Father that sent Me,” and, “The testimony of two men is true,” did He not hold His peace, instead of adding, “I am One that bear witness of Myself”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It was evidently to show His independence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And He placeth Himself first; “I am One that bear witness of Myself.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Here He showeth His equality of honor, and that they were profited nothing by saying that they knew God the Father, while they knew not Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And He saith that the cause of this (ignorance) was that they were not willing to know Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Therefore He telleth them that it was not possible to know the Father without knowing Him, that even so He might draw them to the knowledge of Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For since leaving Him they even sought to get the knowledge of the Father, He saith, “Ye cannot know the Father without Me.” ( Ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So that they who blaspheme the Son, blaspheme not the Son only, but Him that begat Him also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This let us avoid, and glorify the Son.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Had He not been of the same Nature, He would not have spoken thus.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For had He merely taught, but been of different Substance, a man might not have known Him, and yet have known the Father; and again, it would not have been that one who knew Him, would have altogether known the Father; for neither doth one who knoweth a man know an Angel.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Yes,” replieth some one, “he that knoweth the creation, knoweth God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: By no means.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Many, or rather I should say, all men know the creation, (for they see it,) but they know not God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us then glorify the Son of God, not with this glory (of words) only, but that also which is by works.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the first without the last is nothing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Behold,” saith St. Paul, “thou art called a Jew, and restest in the Law, and makest thy boast of God—thou therefore that teachest another, teachest thou not thyself?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Thou that makest thy boast of the Law, through breaking of the Law dishonorest thou God?” ( Rom. ii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Beware lest we also who make boast of the rightness of our faith dishonor God by not manifesting a life agreeable to the faith, causing Him to be blasphemed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For He would have the Christian to be the teacher of the world, its leaven, its salt, its light.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And what is that light?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It is a life which shineth, and hath in it no dark thing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Light is not useful to itself, nor leaven, nor salt, but showeth its usefulness towards others, and so we are required to do good, not to ourselves only, but to others.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For salt, if it salt not, is not salt.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Moreover another thing is evident, that if we be righteous, others shall certainly be so also; but as long as we are not righteous, we shall not be able to assist others.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let there be nothing foolish or silly among us; such are worldly matters, such are the cares of this life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore the virgins were called foolish, because they were busy about foolish, worldly matters, gathering things together here, but laying not up treasure where they ought.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Fear there is lest this be our case, fear lest we too depart clothed with filthy garments, to that place where all have them bright and shining.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For nothing is more filthy, nothing more impure, than sin.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore the Prophet declaring its nature cried out, “My wounds stink, and are corrupt.” ( Ps. xxxviii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And if thou wilt fully learn how ill-savored sin is, consider it after it hath been done; when thou art delivered from the desire, when the fire no longer troubleth thee, then shalt thou see what sin is.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Consider anger, when thou art calm; consider avarice, when thou dost not feel it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: There is nothing more shameful, nothing more accursed, than rapine and avarice.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This we continually say, desiring not to vex you, but to gain some great and wonderful advantage.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For he who hath not acted rightly after hearing once, may perhaps do so after hearing a second time; and he who hath passed by the second time, may do right after the third.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: God grant that we, being delivered from all evil things, may have the sweet savor of Christ; for to Him, with the Father and the Holy Ghost is glory, now and ever and world without end.
